name: Peppernut Pear Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare a 10-inch tart tin, preferably 1.5 inches deep with a removable base; some baking parchment; dried baking beans.
2. Preheat the oven to gas mark 5, 375°F, 190°C.
3. Process flour, salt, and sugar for a few seconds.
4. Roughly chop butter and add to the flour mixture; process for about 15 seconds until crumb-like.
5. Whisk egg yolk with water in a small bowl, then add to the flour and butter mixture.
6. Process for about 30 seconds or until the pastry is clinging in a solid mass around the central spindle.
7. Shape the pastry into a flat round and wrap in clingfilm; refrigerate for about 30 minutes or overnight.
8. Grease the tart tin thoroughly, focusing on where base and sides meet. Roll out pastry to chamois leather thickness.
9. Line the tart tin without stretching, starting from the center and draping outward.
10. Prick the bottom with a fork; place in oven for 15 minutes until set and golden. Remove, return crust to oven for 3 more minutes, then cool.
11. Process biscuits into coarse crumbs and combine with ground hazlenuts in a bowl.
12. Cream butter and sugar in food processor until smooth.
13. Beat eggs in a small bowl; add nut and crumb mixture, processing briefly until incorporated.
14. Continue adding nut and crumb mixture, then egg, until mixed but not over-processed.
15. Add ginger wine or liqueur.
16. Peel pears, cut into halves, remove cores, make lengthwise cuts, leaving base intact.
17. Fill pastry case with frangipane; place pears on top with stalks pointing to center.
18. Bake for 25 minutes at gas mark 3, 325°F, 170°C until golden brown and just set (creamier texture if slightly soft in the center).
19. Serve warm or chilled with a dollop of crème fraîche.
